VERY BRIEF LANDSPOUT TORNADO REPORTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF FM 2082 AND HILLARD ROAD JUST NORTHWEST OF LAKE STAMFORD. TORNADO AFFECTED RURAL AREAS AND NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED
SCATTERED REPORTS OF WIND DAMAGE FROM SUB SEVERE WIND GUSTS INCLUDING ONE POWER POLE...SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES...AND SOME PLAYGROUND EQUIPMENT.
FLOODING REPORTED ACROSS CITY INCLUDING MILL POND PARK...FM 500...AND LIVE OAK ST.

*** 1 INJ *** A MICROBURST OCCURRED IN THE INDIAN CREEK NEIGHBORHOOD IN GRAPE CREEK NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GLASS ROAD AND GRAPE CREEK ROAD. SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF OF SEVERAL RESIDENCES AND A SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS MOVED OFF ITS BLOCKS. SEVERAL WINDOWS OF MOBILE HOMES AND VEHICLES WERE BROKEN BY FLYING DEBRIS AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. A LARGE METAL BUILDING HOUSING LARGE HAIL BALES WAS ALSO DESTROYED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80 TO 90 MPH.

DAMAGING THUNDERSTORMS WINDS REMOVED ALL OF THE ROOF DECKING FROM A RESIDENCE... A HAY BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED...A 100 YEAR OLD OAK TREE WAS TOPPLED...AND TWO TELEPHONE POLES WERE DOWNED BY THE STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
TWO TREE REPORTED DOWN IN CROSS PLAINS
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN OR UPROOTED. THE LARGEST TREE WAS 28 INCHES IN DIAMETER...AND 30 FEET LONG. THE TREE IS BLOCKING THE ROAD. POWER LINES WERE ALSO DOWN.
NUMEROUS LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN. A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED.
SEVERAL CLOSED ROADS WERE REPORTED IN THE CITY OF ABILENE... INCLUDING MOCKINGBIRD UNDERPASS... SOUTH 27TH FROM TREADWAY TO JACKSON STREET... AND SOUTH 32ND FROM TREADWAY TO JACKSON STREET.
SEVERAL CARS REPORTED STALLED IN THE CITY DUE TO HIGH WATER FROM FLASH FLOODING.
A CAR WAS REPORTED STALLED DUE TO HIGH WATER AT THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 142 AND COUNTY ROAD 134.
A FEW MESQUITE LIMBS DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 36. THE LIMBS WERE 3 TO 5 FEET IN LENGTH.
A FEW LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN. THE LIMBS WERE AT LEAST 10 FEET IN LENGTH.
ROOF TORN OFF OF HOME...DAMAGE TO INTERIOR WALLS...CARPORT DESTROYED...AND DAMAGE TO PORCH FROM STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS.
